deterministic_steps: Sort receipts by source_family_id, canonical_subject_id, observed_at, and receipt_id.
Join by explicit receipt_id and fragment_id first; fall back only to exact canonical subject and section keys.
Emit supported claim refs only when every required claim field has a receipt-backed fragment.
Emit candidate or gap states for partial joins; never synthesize missing claim text.

inputs.description: First-party source receipts with receipt_id, source_family_id, observed_at, and support_state.
inputs.name: source_receipts

inputs.description: Extracted text or metadata fragments already tied to source_receipt IDs and stable section keys.
inputs.name: evidence_fragments

inputs.description: Structured candidate claims generated from deterministic packet templates, not free-form inference.
inputs.name: claim_candidates

outputs.description: Claim references that carry the exact receipt IDs and fragment IDs used as proof.
outputs.name: joined_claim_refs

outputs.description: Candidates that could not be joined to proof and therefore cannot become public claims.
outputs.name: rejected_claim_candidates

outputs.description: Known gaps for missing receipts, missing sections, ambiguous subjects, or blocked sources.
outputs.name: coverage_gaps

blueprints.purpose: Join artifact claim candidates to first-party evidence fragments using explicit receipt and subject keys before any public claim can be emitted.

deterministic_steps: Normalize dates to local calendar days and half-open intervals [start, end).
Union overlapping observed windows per source family after sorting by start date, end date, and receipt_id.
Intersect observed windows with required windows and sum covered weighted days.
Emit missing or stale intervals as known gaps; no uncovered interval becomes an absence claim.

inputs.description: Requested half-open date windows with source_family_id and required coverage weight.
inputs.name: required_windows

inputs.description: Receipt-backed half-open date windows with valid_from, valid_to, and receipt IDs.
inputs.name: observed_windows

inputs.description: Deterministic evaluation date supplied by the caller or release capsule.
inputs.name: as_of_date

outputs.description: Weighted coverage ratio from 0.0 to 1.0 with formula ID and input window IDs.
outputs.name: coverage_score

outputs.description: Receipt-backed covered date segments after deterministic interval union.
outputs.name: covered_segments

outputs.description: Known gaps for uncovered, stale, or blocked date segments.
outputs.name: missing_or_stale_segments

blueprints.purpose: Score how much of a requested period is covered by first-party evidence without treating uncovered time as proof that no event occurred.

deterministic_steps: Compute non-negative age_days from as_of_date minus observed_at date.
Look up source_family freshness_sla_days; unknown SLA emits a freshness gap.
Score each receipt by linear decay and weighted mean by source family.
Sort stale receipts by oldest observed_at, source_family_id, and receipt_id.

inputs.description: First-party receipts with observed_at timestamps and source family IDs.
inputs.name: source_receipts

inputs.description: Configured maximum age per source family before a receipt is stale.
inputs.name: freshness_sla_days

inputs.description: Deterministic evaluation timestamp supplied by the caller or release capsule.
inputs.name: as_of_date

numerical_method.formula: receipt_score = max(0, 1 - age_days / freshness_sla_days); score = weighted_mean(receipt_score)

outputs.description: Weighted score from 0.0 to 1.0 with age days, SLA days, and receipt IDs.
outputs.name: freshness_score

outputs.description: Receipts inside the configured freshness window.
outputs.name: fresh_receipts

outputs.description: Receipts outside the configured freshness window, emitted as gaps before final use.
outputs.name: stale_receipts

blueprints.purpose: Score receipt freshness against configured source-family service windows using only supplied observed_at metadata.

deterministic_steps: Normalize supplied public names and identifiers with configured exact rules before scoring.
Score exact registration-number matches above corporate-number matches, name matches, and alias matches.
Emit candidate matches only when public record receipts exist and private raw values remain absent.
Keep unmatched CSV-derived facts tenant-private and hash-only; never export raw rows.

inputs.description: Minimized tenant-private facts such as hashed counterparty keys, amount buckets, and provider family.
inputs.name: private_counterparty_fingerprints

inputs.description: Receipt-backed public records such as invoice registry or company profile rows already supplied.
inputs.name: public_counterparty_records

inputs.description: Deterministic alias keys from first-party or tenant-approved configuration.
inputs.name: allowed_alias_table

outputs.description: Candidate public-record matches with score, reason code, receipt IDs, and private value fingerprints only.
outputs.name: counterparty_match_candidates

outputs.description: Private fact fingerprints that did not reach candidate threshold.
outputs.name: unmatched_private_facts

outputs.description: Public source checks that can be cited independently of private CSV content.
outputs.name: public_record_checks

blueprints.purpose: Match minimized tenant-private accounting CSV-derived counterparty facts to supplied public records as candidate checks, while preventing private facts from becoming public proof.

deterministic_steps: Map applicant facts to requirement keys only when both sides have explicit normalized keys.
Classify each requirement signal as matched, missing, unknown, conflict, or professional_review_required.
Unknown and conflict signals reduce triage confidence but never become negative verdicts.
Emit candidate_for_review and questions_for_professional instead of eligibility or compliance conclusions.

inputs.description: Minimized applicant facts supplied by the user or derived from tenant-private CSV buckets.
inputs.name: applicant_profile_facts

inputs.description: Receipt-backed requirement, exclusion, deadline, and document rules from first-party evidence.
inputs.name: program_or_regulation_requirements

inputs.description: Configured signal weights and mandatory review gates for candidate ranking.
inputs.name: triage_policy

numerical_method.formula: score = sum(matched_signal_weight) / sum(known_required_signal_weight); unknown and conflict signals are reported separately

outputs.description: Requirement signals marked matched, missing, unknown, conflict, or professional_review_required.
outputs.name: triage_signals

outputs.description: Ranked candidate item for human review; explicitly not an eligibility or compliance verdict.
outputs.name: candidate_for_review

outputs.description: Deterministic questions generated from missing, unknown, or conflicting source-backed signals.
outputs.name: questions_for_professional

blueprints.purpose: Rank source-backed requirement signals for professional review without asserting subsidy eligibility, regulatory compliance, or accounting treatment.

deterministic_steps: Compute days_until_due from as_of_date and due_at using local calendar days.
Compute buffer_ratio from preparation_days_by_action divided by max(days_until_due, 1).
Add freshness penalty from source freshness state and missing-date penalty from gap state.
Sort by descending risk score, then earliest due_at, then source_family_id and receipt_id.

inputs.description: Receipt-backed deadlines with due_at, source_family_id, and required action labels.
inputs.name: deadline_records

inputs.description: Configured minimum preparation days per action type.
inputs.name: preparation_days_by_action

inputs.description: Deterministic evaluation date supplied by the caller or release capsule.
inputs.name: as_of_date

numerical_method.formula: risk_score = clamp(0, 100, urgency_points + buffer_pressure_points + freshness_penalty_points + missing_data_penalty_points)

outputs.description: Deadline records sorted by risk score with formula ID, receipt IDs, and tie-break trace.
outputs.name: ranked_deadline_risks

outputs.description: Known gaps or warnings when a due date is missing, stale, overdue, or inside prep buffer.
outputs.name: overdue_or_imminent_gaps

outputs.description: Non-advisory internal scheduling suggestions keyed to source-backed due dates.
outputs.name: calendar_hold_suggestions

blueprints.purpose: Rank receipt-backed deadlines by deterministic urgency, preparation buffer, and source freshness so artifacts can prioritize review work without promising outcomes.

deterministic_steps: Compare checked_scope to required_scope by exact source family, filter, and time-window keys.
Emit a no_hit_lease only for the checked scope and configured expiry window.
Emit unchecked_scope_gaps for any required scope outside the observed no-hit query.
Disable absence claims in every public and internal no-hit output.

inputs.description: Exact query scope, source families, filters, and observed_at timestamp that produced no hit.
inputs.name: checked_scope

inputs.description: Configured source families and filters expected for the requested artifact.
inputs.name: required_scope

inputs.description: Configured expiry duration for reusing a no-hit observation.
inputs.name: lease_policy

outputs.description: Scoped lease stating what was checked, when it expires, and that absence claims are disabled.
outputs.name: no_hit_lease

outputs.description: Known gaps for source families, filters, or periods outside the checked scope.
outputs.name: unchecked_scope_gaps

outputs.description: Deterministic follow-up source families or filters needed before artifact completion.
outputs.name: next_checks

blueprints.purpose: Represent no-hit observations as scoped search results with leases and gaps, never as proof that a record, program, obligation, or event does not exist.
